01-why-multiple-agents.instruction: Give 3 scenarios where one agent isn't enough. For each, explain why a single agent would fail.
01-why-multiple-agents.successMessage: Knowing when to go multi-agent is the first architecture decision.

02-agent-roles-and-specialization.instruction: Design 4 specialized agent roles for e-commerce: what each owns, its tools, how it interfaces with others.
02-agent-roles-and-specialization.successMessage: Clear role boundaries prevent overlap. Solid design.

03-communication-patterns.instruction: Compare 3 inter-agent communication patterns: direct messaging, shared blackboard, event bus. Pros/cons for each.
03-communication-patterns.successMessage: Communication patterns are the nervous system of multi-agent architectures.

04-orchestration-architectures.instruction: Centralized (one orchestrator) vs decentralized (peer-to-peer) agents. When to use each and failure modes.
04-orchestration-architectures.successMessage: The most important multi-agent architecture decision.

05-shared-memory-and-state.instruction: Design shared state for 5 agents: read/write without conflicts. Include locking, consistency, conflict resolution.
05-shared-memory-and-state.successMessage: Hardest problem in distributed systems. You've thought it through.

06-conflict-resolution.instruction: Design a voting protocol for 3 disagreeing agents. How votes are weighted, tie-breaking, human override trigger.
06-conflict-resolution.successMessage: Democratic agent decisions with human override. Responsible design.

07-scaling-agent-systems.instruction: Your system handles 100 tasks/day, needs to handle 10,000. What changes in architecture, infrastructure, monitoring?

08-human-oversight-patterns.instruction: Design 3 oversight levels: full autonomy, approval required, human-in-the-loop. What triggers each level.
08-human-oversight-patterns.successMessage: Graduated oversight is responsible autonomous deployment.

09-real-world-multi-agent-systems.instruction: Write a prompt for Claude to design a multi-agent customer support system: roles, escalation, knowledge sharing, metrics.
09-real-world-multi-agent-systems.successMessage: Customer support is the perfect multi-agent use case.

10-building-your-agent-team.instruction: Final project: complete multi-agent system for YOUR project. Agent roster, communication, shared state, oversight, deployment.
